commonMistakes: Putting every local input into Redux.
Performing side effects inside a reducer.
Mutating props or state instead of producing the next value.
Adding state or an Effect for a value that can be derived during render.

contentAuthority.canonical: New JS2Next Release 1 curriculum and independently authored content.
contentAuthority.courseTranscripts: Coverage and sequencing research only; never learner-facing authority.
contentAuthority.legacyProjectContent: Supplementary internal reference only; excluded from runtime unless explicitly re-authored and approved.

learningObjectives.text: Explain store using a new example and justify the result.
Explain reducers using a new example and justify the result.
Explain actions using a new example and justify the result.
Trace and explain unidirectional state flow using a new example and justify the result.

mentalModel: Draw a decision map: **Store** → **Reducers** → **Actions** → **Unidirectional state flow**. Add branches where the learner must choose an alternative and label the evidence for the choice.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Store.

choices.feedback: Store is best understood as follows: A Redux store holds the application state tree and coordinates dispatch, reducers, subscriptions, and middleware.
choices.label: A Redux store holds the application state tree and coordinates dispatch, reducers, subscriptions, and middleware.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Store.
choices.label: Values calculable from props and state should usually be derived during render rather than stored separately. Duplicate state creates synchronization bugs.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Redux Fundamentals.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Redux Fundamentals.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Redux Fundamentals.
choices.label: Start with a framework abstraction and avoid checking the underlying language behavior.

choices.feedback: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
choices.label: First identify the requirement, then apply the relevant rule from Store, inspect the result, and only then refactor or optimize.

questions.explanation: The reliable sequence starts from the requirement, applies a known rule, observes behavior, and improves the solution afterward.
questions.hint: Prefer a sequence that makes failures visible early.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Redux Fundamentals.
choices.label: Choose based only on line count, without considering readability, correctness, or future change.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Redux Fundamentals.

choices.feedback: This does not match the lesson's model. Reconsider the role of Redux Fundamentals.
choices.label: Avoid Redux Fundamentals completely because all abstractions reduce maintainability.

choices.feedback: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
choices.label: Use Redux Fundamentals when it makes intent, ownership, or data flow clearer; avoid adding it mechanically when a simpler, equally explicit solution is sufficient.

questions.explanation: Engineering judgment balances clarity and cost. The concept should serve the requirement rather than become an end in itself.
questions.hint: Choose the option that acknowledges both benefits and trade-offs.
questions.prompt: Which guideline shows the best judgment about when to use Redux Fundamentals?

researchSources.title: Introduction to Redux
researchSources.usage: Coverage research only; no transcript wording is canonical.

sections.body: A Redux store holds the application state tree and coordinates dispatch, reducers, subscriptions, and middleware.
sections.title: Store

sections.body: Redux reducers calculate next state from current state and an action. They must be predictable and side-effect free.
sections.title: Reducers

sections.body: Redux actions are plain event descriptions with a type and optional payload.
sections.title: Actions

sections.body: UI dispatches an action, reducers calculate state, and subscribed UI reads the new state.
sections.title: Unidirectional state flow

sections.explanation: An action describes an event and the reducer calculates the next state.
sections.title: Worked example

summary: Redux Fundamentals connects Store, Reducers, Actions.
Use the topic to make behavior and decisions explicit rather than memorizing isolated syntax.
Verify understanding by explaining a new example and answering the topic quiz without looking back.
title: Redux Fundamentals

topicQuiz.failurePolicy: Show explanations, add missed objectives to local review, and allow immediate or later retry.

whyThisMatters: Learners frequently use redux fundamentals without a clear model of why it behaves as it does; this lesson makes that model explicit. It belongs to the **Redux** module.
